page: Microsoft Azure E64as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Eav6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong compute and memory capabilities. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Geekbench image processing. It also registers strong performance in multi-core stress-ng, Passmark CPU tests, and Redis SET operations, which reach 88.78 million operations per second. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is average at 154.03 GB/sec, while cached small-block read bandwidth is strong at 4863.52 GB/sec.

The absence of local storage requires reliance on external Premium Storage, representing an architectural tradeoff for disk-heavy operations. However, the high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation provide qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of physical hardware management. This server is suited for database operations, memory-cached applications, software compilation, and moderate LLM inference tasks, as demonstrated by its top-tier prompt processing speed of 4447.51 tokens/second for small models.
description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eav6 family, powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench multi-core metrics, and image processing. Uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is average at 154.03 GB/sec, but cached small-block reads and uncached large-block writes perform strongly. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy enterprise applications by providing high resource density. It is suited for database operations, large-scale software compilation, and memory-cached web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor, delivering top-tier single-core and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high RAM density for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64 vCPUs and 3.7 GHz clock speed
512.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
Top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks
No local storage included, requiring external Premium Storage solutions
Suited for database operations, memory caching, and software compilation
